design_philosophy.core_loop: Raise and educate Pino (a Pinocchio-like robot boy) by collecting items, managing dual health bars (physical and mental), completing character-building challenges, and activating virtue circuits through parameter-based success conditions
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Balancing Pino's physical and mental wellbeing while developing his character parameters (strength, kindness, etc.) to succeed in virtue challenges that determine his humanity
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Become a nurturing caretaker/educator responsible for transforming a robot into a human-like being through moral choices, training, and relationship-building
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Combines Pinocchio narrative with interactive parenting mechanics (praise/scold system); uses point-and-click adventure interface for a life sim; emphasizes character education over combat; dual health system reflects psychological and physical needs; final goal is fostering human-robot relations rather than traditional victory

difficulty.checkpointing: Non-linear progression with daily cycle system; sleep mechanic allows health restoration at monetary/time cost; no traditional checkpoint system documented
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency: player controls item collection, parameter training pace, health management decisions, and which virtue circuits to activate. Success conditions are parameter-dependent rather than mechanical skill-based. Secret ending requires speedrun-style replays within time limits

progression.upgrade_system: Character parameter development through item collection (consumables like pudding for trust, batteries for health) and task completion. Parameters unlock access to virtue circuits; 'Circuit J' is final gate
progression.unlock_mechanism: Virtue circuits activated by succeeding at character-specific challenges; final circuit unlocks based on achieving emotional/moral development thresholds. Secret ending requires completing acts within specific day limits on replay
replayability_hooks: Secret ending only accessible via speedrun-style replays with day limits per act
Multiple virtue circuit paths suggest different character development strategies
Farming and sailing tasks have time-based progression encouraging multiple playthroughs

world_building_techniques: Point-and-click dialogue interface with Tinker (fairy companion) mimics adventure game narrative delivery
Character behavior learning system (scolding/praising) creates emergent relationship narrative
Director drew from anime sources (Sekai Meisaku Gekijō, Toei Animation) for narrative tone and world aesthetics
Environmental tasks (sowing seeds, sword fighting) teach through experience rather than exposition
Pino's extensive animation library reinforces character personality and growth
Ending encourages continuation, suggesting narrative completeness is variable

faction_indicators: Human-robot relations as central theme (final task goal)
Gijin (robot-like beings) as distinct social category
danger_signals: Mental health depletion through offense/psychological challenge
Physical health risk through combat/physical tasks
Implicit tension between robot nature and human development expectations
environmental_storytelling: Item types signal available teaching methods (seeds for farming education, sword for combat training, books for mental development)
Day-cycle system grounds experience in temporal progression and consequence
Sleep mechanic creates resource tension: health vs. time/money economy
Two-health-bar system visually communicates holistic character development philosophy
